Upon a quick search, I find that this problem is similar to LeetCode problem 1195, which is about the minimum number of swaps using adjacent swaps to make a string a palindrome.

Looking up the solution for that problem, I find that the approach is to use a two-pointer method, where for each mismatch, we find the minimal number of swaps by considering the closest matching character on either side.

The solution involves:

- For each pair of left and right pointers, if the characters match, move inward.

- If not, find the next occurrence of s[right] in the left half, and calculate the cost as (j - left). Alternatively, find the next occurrence of s[left] in the right half, and calculate the cost as (right - j). Choose the minimal of the two, add to the result, and move the pointers inward.

So, the code would look something like this:

int minMovesToMakePalindrome(string s) {
    int n = s.size();
    int left = 0, right = n - 1;
    int res = 0;
    while (left < right) {
        if (s[left] == s[right]) {
            left++;
            right--;
        } else {
            int pos_left = right;
            // Find the first occurrence of s[right] in s[left+1 ... right]
            for (int j = left + 1; j <= right; j++) {
                if (s[j] == s[right]) {
                    pos_left = j;
                    break;
                }
            }
            // Find the first occurrence of s[left] in s[left ... right-1]
            int pos_right = left;
            for (int j = right - 1; j >= left; j--) {
                if (s[j] == s[left]) {
                    pos_right = j;
                    break;
                }
            }
            // Calculate the minimal cost
            int cost_left = pos_left - left;
            int cost_right = right - pos_right;
            res += min(cost_left, cost_right);
            left++;
            right--;
        }
    }
    return res;
}

Looking up the solution for that problem, I find that the approach is to use a two-pointer method, where for each mismatch, we find the minimal number of swaps by considering the closest matching character on either side.

The solution involves:

- For each pair of left and right pointers, if the characters match, move inward.

- If not, find the next occurrence of s[right] in the left half, and calculate the cost as (j - left). Alternatively, find the next occurrence of s[left] in the right half, and calculate the cost as (right - j). Choose the minimal of the two, add to the result, and move the pointers inward.

So, the code would look something like this:

int minMovesToMakePalindrome(string s) {
    int n = s.size();
    int left = 0, right = n - 1;
    int res = 0;
    while (left < right) {
        if (s[left] == s[right]) {
            left++;
            right--;
        } else {
            int pos_left = right;
            // Find the first occurrence of s[right] in s[left+1 ... right]
            for (int j = left + 1; j <= right; j++) {
                if (s[j] == s[right]) {
                    pos_left = j;
                    break;
                }
            }
            // Find the first occurrence of s[left] in s[left ... right-1]
            int pos_right = left;
            for (int j = right - 1; j >= left; j--) {
                if (s[j] == s[left]) {
                    pos_right = j;
                    break;
                }
            }
            // Calculate the minimal cost
            int cost_left = pos_left - left;
            int cost_right = right - pos_right;
            res += min(cost_left, cost_right);
            left++;
            right--;
        }
    }
    return res;
}
